question: What is the overall architecture of this repository? I want a clear picture of: (1) the tech stack (backend language/framework, frontend framework, database, message bus if any), (2) the top-level directory layout, (3) how the backend and frontend communicate (API style, auth), and (4) what the core domain abstractions are (tenants, users, memberships, plans, branding, billing, etc.). List the most important files/packages for each area.
answer:
LastSaaS is designed as a comprehensive SaaS boilerplate with a clear separation between its backend and frontend components.

### 1. Tech Stack
*   **Backend Language/Framework:** Go 1.25, using [`gorilla/mux`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L299) for routing (see [`go.mod`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FDockerfile#L5) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/go.mod`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fgo.mod)).
*   **Frontend Framework:** React 19 with TypeScript, Vite 7, and Tailwind CSS 4.
*   **Database:** MongoDB (Atlas or local), as stated in the [Database Management and Schema](#database-management-and-schema) wiki section and [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/go.mod`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fgo.mod).
*   **Message Bus/Event System:** The backend uses an internal event emitter ([`internal/events`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L21)) that drives an outgoing webhook system ([`internal/webhooks`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L32)). This system supports 19 event types across various categories (billing, team, user lifecycle, etc.), with HMAC-SHA256 signing for integrity (see [Core SaaS Feature Set](#project-overview-and-architecture-core-saas-feature-set) and [Go Backend Architecture](#project-overview-and-architecture-go-backend-architecture)).

### 2. Top-Level Directory Layout
The repository has a straightforward top-level structure:
*   [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend): Contains all Go-related code for the API, business logic, and database interactions.
    *   [`cmd/server/main.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L883): The main entry point for the HTTP server.
    *   `cmd/lastsaas/main.go`: CLI administration tool and MCP server.
    *   [`internal/`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L496): Core backend services and logic (e.g., [`api/handlers`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L497), [`auth`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fauth%2Fjwt.go#L1), [`db`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe%2Fstripe.go#L33), [`models`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L1), [`stripe`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fconfig%2Fconfig.go#L22)).
    *   [`config/`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L457): Configuration files (e.g., [`dev.yaml`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L459), [`prod.yaml`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L459)).
*   [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/frontend`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend): Houses the React/TypeScript application.
    *   [`src/`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L514): Source code for the frontend.
        *   [`api/client.ts`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L515): Centralized API client.
        *   [`components/`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L516): Reusable UI components.
        *   [`contexts/`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L517): React Contexts for global state.
        *   [`pages/`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L884): Application pages organized by section (admin, app, auth, public).
*   [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/scripts`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fscripts): Utility scripts.
*   [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/Dockerfile`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FDockerfile): Multi-stage Docker build for both backend and frontend.
*   `/jonradoff/lastsaas/fly.toml`: Fly.io deployment configuration.
*   `/jonradoff/lastsaas/VERSION`: Project version.
(See the [Project Structure](https://github.com/jonradoff/lastsaas/blob/main/README.md#project-structure) section in the [`README.md`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fmanifest.json#L17) and the [Project Overview and Architecture](#project-overview-and-architecture) wiki section).

### 3. Backend and Frontend Communication
*   **API Style:** The backend exposes a RESTful API with endpoints for various functionalities like authentication, tenant management, billing, and administration (see [Backend API Endpoints](#backend-api-endpoints)). The API documentation is dynamically generated and available at [`/api/docs`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L232) (HTML) and [`/api/docs/openapi.json`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fopenapi.go#L93) (OpenAPI 3.0 spec).
*   **Authentication:** Communication is secured primarily using JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) for session management, along with refresh token rotation. OAuth 2.0 integrations with providers like Google, GitHub, and Microsoft are also supported. API keys with [`admin`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L174) or [`user`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fuser.go#L20) authority scopes can also be used for authentication (see [User Authentication and Authorization](#user-authentication-and-authorization) and [Core SaaS Feature Set](#project-overview-and-architecture-core-saas-feature-set)).
    *   **Frontend API Client:** All frontend API interactions are centralized through an [`axios`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L515) instance at [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/frontend/src/api/client.ts`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fapi%2Fclient.ts). This client dynamically sets authentication tokens and tenant IDs in request headers and includes an automated token refresh mechanism (see [Frontend API Client and Data Fetching](#frontend-api-client-and-data-fetching)).
*   **Middleware:** The backend utilizes HTTP middleware for cross-cutting concerns such as request management, security, multi-tenancy context, authorization (including role-based access control), and rate limiting. A bootstrap guard ensures the system is initialized before accessing certain routes (see [HTTP Server and API Gateway](#http-server-and-api-gateway)).

### 4. Core Domain Abstractions
The backend defines a comprehensive set of data models ([`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/models`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels)) for its multi-tenant SaaS capabilities, including validation tags for data integrity (see [Core Data Models and Validation](#core-data-models-and-validation)). Key abstractions include:

*   **Users:** Defined in `models/user.go`, managing user details, authentication methods ([`AuthMethodPassword`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fuser.go#L12), [`AuthMethodGoogle`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fuser.go#L13), [`AuthMethodGitHub`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fuser.go#L14), [`AuthMethodMicrosoft`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fuser.go#L15), [`AuthMethodMagicLink`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fuser.go#L16)), and MFA status. The [`AuthHandler`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fauth.go#L30) ([`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/auth.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fauth.go)) manages user-related API interactions.
*   **Tenants:** Represented by the [`Tenant`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftenant.go#L9) struct in `models/tenant.go`, capturing tenant-specific information like name, slug, billing details, and root status. Multi-tenancy context is handled by [`middleware.NewTenantMiddleware`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L255).
*   **Memberships:** The [`TenantMembership`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fmembership.go#L17) struct in `models/membership.go` defines a user's role within a tenant, with [`MemberRole`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fmembership.go#L9) constants ([`owner`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L60), [`admin`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L174), [`user`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fuser.go#L20)) defining permission levels. Handled by [`middleware.RequireRole`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L549).
*   **Plans:** The [`Plan`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L37) struct in `models/plan.go` defines subscription plans, entitlements, and billing cycles. Managed by [`plansHandler`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L347) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/plans.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fplans.go).
*   **Branding:** [`BrandingConfig`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L22) in `models/branding.go` stores application branding elements like logos, themes, and custom pages. The [`BrandingContext`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FBrandingContext.tsx#L40) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/frontend/src/contexts/BrandingContext.tsx`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FBrandingContext.tsx) handles this on the frontend.
*   **Billing:** Handled by models in `models/billing.go` (e.g., [`BillingStatus`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftenant.go#L20), [`FinancialTransaction`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbilling.go#L29)) and `models/stripe_mapping.go`. Integrates with Stripe via [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/stripe`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe) for subscriptions, trials, and credit bundles (see [Stripe Billing and Payments Integration](#stripe-billing-and-payments-integration)). The [`billingHandler`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L351) in `/jonradof/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/billing.go` provides API endpoints.
*   **API Keys:** The [`APIKey`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fapi_key.go#L20) struct in `models/api_key.go` defines the structure of API keys and their authority levels ([`admin`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L174), [`user`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fuser.go#L20)). Managed by [`apiKeysHandler`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L359) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/apikeys.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fapikeys.go).
*   **Webhooks:** The [`Webhook`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fwebhook.go#L81) struct in `models/webhook.go` manages webhook configurations and delivery logs. The [`webhookDispatcher`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L249) ([`internal/webhooks`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L32)) handles event dispatching.
*   **System Configuration:** The [`SystemConfig`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fsystem.go#L9) struct in `models/system.go` holds core system settings, and [`ConfigVar`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fconfig_var.go#L18) in `models/config_var.go` defines individual configurable variables. Managed by [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/configstore`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fconfigstore) and the [`configHandler`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L346) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/config.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fconfig.go).

Frontend global state is managed through React Contexts ([`/jonradoff/lastsaas/frontend/src/contexts`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts)), including [`AuthContext`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FAuthContext.tsx#L24), [`BrandingContext`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FBrandingContext.tsx#L40), [`TenantContext`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FTenantContext.tsx#L13), and [`ThemeContext`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FThemeContext.tsx#L14) (see [Frontend Global State Contexts](#frontend-global-state-contexts)).
